title: Veronica bullii
taxonomy: Veronica bullii is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a type of speedwell, a genus of plants that are commonly found in alpine and subalpine regions. The exact classification of Veronica bullii can be further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Lamiales. This plant is native to the alpine regions of Europe and Asia. Its classification is primarily based on its morphology and genetic characteristics.

intro: Veronica bullii, also known as Veronica's cat's-eye or Veronica bullii, is a perennial plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) native to Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Veronica bullii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which have been found to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Veronica bullii is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in topical creams and ointments for its skin-soothing and antiseptic effects.
